critic.overallAssessment: Strong sightseeing and listings coverage: all eight overnight towns have 3-5 food and sleep options, the POI set (72 waypoints, stave churches, summer farms, Besseggen, glacier walks, Stegastein) is rich, and headline logistics topics are present. What's missing is the operational layer that decides whether the trip actually works: nothing on water/resupply across the two remote plateau crossings (Skabu-Bygdin and Stolsvidda), no snow/storm fallback for Finse-Flam even though snow typically lingers there to mid-July, no booking-urgency triage despite being four weeks from departure in peak season, and no 2026-specific checks (July rail works that would strand bikes on replacement buses; Vinjerock 16-19 July loading the Bygdin corridor). Bike security during the Besseggen and fjord-cruise days and camper-specific detail (legal tent spots, last supermarket before Rallarvegen, non-guest dinners at Finse) are also absent. Fix the six gaps above and the plan moves from a good guidebook to an executable itinerary.

findings.title: Haugseter Fjellstue confirmed open daily for your crossing: 19 Jun-27 Sep 2026, restaurant 11:00-20:00
findings.detail: Haugseter's own site confirms 2026 season dates 19 June to 27 September, restaurant open daily 11:00-20:00 with lunch from 11:00 and dinner from 15:00, so a Friday 10 July pass-through works with no day-of-week restrictions published (https://www.haugseter.no/apningstider/). It sits at 1,044 m on the north side of Vinstervatn near the western (Bygdin) end of the 45 km Jotunheimvegen, roughly 6-8 km before the Fv51 junction, meaning riding Skåbu to Bygdin you cover ~35-38 km with no services before reaching it (https://ut.no/hytte/10817/haugseter-fjellstue, https://www.haugseter.no/sommer/). They serve drop-in food (stølslapper, game, fish from Vinstervatn) and do accommodation if you want a fallback sleep option (https://www.visitbeitostolen.no/en/companies/haugseter-fjellstue/). Phone 61 34 15 10 to double-check on the day.
findings.nearStop: Jotunheimvegen, ~7 km east of Bygdin junction

findings.title: Jotunheimvegen toll: nothing for cyclists to pay (plate-recognition system, cars only), but verify the road is open for the 2026 season
findings.detail: The toll is collected by automatic number-plate recognition billed via youpark.no, with no manned booth, cash box, or Vipps point; the tariff lists motor vehicles only (cars under 3.5t 120 kr, motorcycles 60 kr, mopeds/tractors free, 2024 prices), and bicycles do not appear, so riders pass free with no payment action needed (https://jotunheimvegen.no/bomavgift/). One caveat: as of a June 2026 check, the site's front page still carried last season's closure notice ('stengt for trafikk for 2025'), i.e. not yet updated for the new season; the road normally opens in June (Visit Valdres has noted record-early openings some years), but confirm current status at https://jotunheimvegen.no/ in the week before your 10 July crossing. The same logic applies to the Valdres mountain toll roads (Slettefjellvegen, Panoramavegen): tolls target cars, no published bicycle tariff exists, so budget Vipps/card only for cafes, not bom payments.

findings.title: Lisjordetstølen confirmed open every day 12:00-18:00, 1 July-2 August 2026, including Sunday 12 July
findings.detail: Visit Valdres lists Lisjordetstølen open Monday-Sunday 12:00-18:00 for 1 July to 2 August 2026, serving lapper (pancakes), spekemat (cured meats) and hot/cold drinks, located at Rensenn on Panoramavegen in the middle of Stølsvidda (https://www.valdres.no/opplevelser/lisjordetstolen-p5833243). The farm's own site confirms daily 12-18 all July, with cheese from the local goat dairy and milking demos 18:00-19:00 on selected evenings; it's a working støl with 25 dairy cows, milk production running from ~24 June to mid-August (https://www.lisjordet.no, https://www.facebook.com/Lisjordet). Address Panoramavegen 1641, 2960 Røn, just before Rensenn lake on the route; phone +47 915 70 418. Note it sits on the Vaset-to-Gol leg across Stølsvidda proper (not the Beitostølen-Vaset/Slettefjell day), which on your schedule likely falls on Sunday 12 July: it is explicitly open Sundays, making it your reliable hot-food stop on a day most shops are shut. Coordinates approximate (Rensenn lake area).

findings.title: Langestølen Kolonial og Kafé: open through July as café plus small grocery, but daily hours unpublished, call +47 90 72 24 47
findings.detail: Langestølen is the one grocery-type resupply on the plateau: a seasonal kolonial (small shop) and café directly on Mjølkevegen along Tisleivegen, open 'during the winter holiday, Easter and four months in the summer', so mid-July falls inside the season (https://www.valdres.com/plan-your-stay/eat-and-drink/langestolen-convenient-store-and-cafe-p1661413). Visit Gol's Mjølkevegen guide names it a popular cyclist stop serving rømmegrøt and waffles; they also sell homemade cured sausage, cheese, butter, sour cream, plus fishing permits (https://www.golinfo.no/mjolkevegen-gol-til-vinstra/). No source publishes daily opening hours, so do not bank on it without calling 90 72 24 47 (listed at https://www.1881.no/dagligvare/dagligvare-innlandet/dagligvare-ulnes/langestoelen-kolonial-og-kaf%C3%AA_107010863S10/, which also gives the exact position: Tisleivegen 1680, 2918 Ulnes, 60.8766, 8.9415). If it is open on Sunday 12 July it is effectively your only shop between Beitostølen/Vaset and Gol, since regular Norwegian groceries close Sundays.

findings.title: Water carry plan: 1.5L+ and a half-day of food for each plateau crossing; treat or avoid streams near grazing herds on Stølsvidda
findings.detail: For Skåbu-Bygdin, the SykkelValdres guide is explicit: no fixed food service exists along the Vinstri stretch, carry at least 1.5 litres of water and food for 4-5 hours, and stream water is usually clean above 1,000 m (most of the road) using common sense (https://sykkelvaldres.no/guider/jotunheimvegen-komplett-sykkelguide). Official FHI guidance adds the filter criteria: drink only clear, flowing, odourless water with no grazing livestock or carcasses upstream, and remember risk rises after rain; boiling (or a filter) is the reliable kill step (https://www.fhi.no/sm/drikkevann/rad/for-du-drikker-vann-rett-fra-naturen/). That caveat bites on Stølsvidda and Slettefjell: these are among Norway's most active summer-farm grazing areas (cattle and sheep across the plateau, 25 dairy cows at Lisjordet alone), so on the Beitostølen-Vaset and Vaset-Gol days fill bottles at staffed stops rather than streams: Beitostølen before departure, Gomobu Fjellstue/Vasetstølen at Vaset (Gomobu's summer season runs 1 June-15 August with café serving waffles and hot food, https://www.gomobu.no/arshjul/sommer-pa-fjellet/), then Lisjordetstølen and Langestølen on the plateau. Worst case with everything shut, the carry is manageable: ~2L per rider plus lunch covers the 67 km Beitostølen-Vaset day, and Jotunheimvegen stream water above 1,000 m is the documented backstop on the Skåbu day.

findings.title: Live trail status: Rallarvegrapporten (updated continuously; 2026 has MORE snow than normal)
findings.detail: The authoritative live source is the Rallarvegen report at https://www.haugastol.no/en/rallarvegen-report (Norwegian: https://www.haugastol.no/rallarvegen-report), updated continuously based on weather, snowmelt and plowing. As of the 8 June 2026 update: 'There is still a lot of snow along the full section [Finse-Hallingskeid], more than normal for this time of year' and they 'do not recommend this trip now'; Hallingskeid-Vatnahalsen is already snow-free, Vatnahalsen-Flam open and good. Full route expected passable early July, consistent with haugastol.no's general guidance that the whole road is 'usually passable at the beginning of July' (https://www.haugastol.no/rallarvegen). Haugastol's Finse bike rental opens 4 July 2026 (3 July per the English report). Protocol for the trip: check the report when leaving Vinstra (9 July), again at Geilo (~13-14 July), and call Haugastol directly the day before the Finse-Flam leg on +47 32 08 75 64 or mail@haugastol.no; they also publish webcams. Given the above-normal snowpack, treat 16 July as roughly 50/50 for a clean full ride and pre-plan the train fallback below.

findings.title: Primary fallback: Bergensbanen from Finse to Myrdal, but bike space MUST be reserved in advance (15 spaces, NOK 275)
findings.detail: Vy's rules (https://www.vy.no/en/travelling-with-vy/on-board-the-train/bikes-on-trains and https://www.vy.no/en/customer-service/frequently-asked-questions/what-train-lines-require-bicycle-space-reservations): on the Bergen Line you MUST reserve a bicycle space in advance; 15 spaces per train, bookable at vy.no or in the Vy app under 'Travellers' > 'Bike', or via staffed stations/customer service. On the Bergen Line in summer a fixed bike fare of NOK 275 applies regardless of distance (rallarvegen.com quotes NOK 265 Myrdal-Finse as a price example: https://www.haugastol.no/rallarvegen). Bikes must be labelled with destination, name and phone number (Bergen Line requirement), e-bike batteries carried as hand luggage. Critical caveat: rail replacement buses on the Bergen Line take NO bicycles, so a line closure strands bikes. Practical move: as soon as the week-before forecast looks bad, pre-book bike spaces on a Finse-Myrdal departure on 16 July as insurance (modest sunk cost, ~NOK 275/bike plus passenger fare); all long-distance Bergensbanen trains stop at Finse, several per day in each direction, so Finse is the reliable bail point. There is no separate local train serving Finse; Bane NOR lists only line F4 Oslo S-Bergen at these stations.

findings.title: Mid-route bail at Hallingskeid is nearly useless on a Thursday: one westbound train per day
findings.detail: Bane NOR's station page (https://www.banenor.no/reise-og-trafikk/stasjoner/-h-/hallingskeid/) states only trains 601 and 602 stop at Hallingskeid daily; trains 603, 604, 607 and 608 stop additionally only on Fridays and Sundays. 16 July 2026 is a Thursday, so effectively one westbound departure (601, Oslo-Bergen direction, stops at Myrdal) serves Hallingskeid that day, and bike space on it must already be reserved (Bergen Line rule). The station is unstaffed, has no road access, sits inside a snow shed at 1,110 m with a 24-hour waiting room and WC, and Bane NOR explicitly warns that if trains are cancelled there is NO alternative transport from Hallingskeid. Planning implication: make the go/no-go call at Finse, not mid-leg. If you do get pinned at Hallingskeid, the DNT self-service cabin there (mentioned on https://en.flamsbana.no/rallarvegen and https://www.haugastol.no/rallarvegen) is the overnight option; note Hallingskeid-Vatnahalsen was already snow-free by 8 June 2026, so the snow risk is concentrated on the Finse-Hallingskeid 19 km.

findings.title: Flamsbana from Myrdal takes bikes: 15 reservable spaces, reservation mandatory, ~NOK 280
findings.detail: Per Vy (https://www.vy.no/en/customer-service/frequently-asked-questions/what-train-lines-require-bicycle-space-reservations): The Flam Railway has 15 bicycle spaces available for reservation, reservation is mandatory, and bike spaces can only be reserved during the summer season when Rallarvegen is open between Myrdal and Flam. Book with the passenger ticket at vy.no/Vy app under 'Travellers' > 'Bike'. Price example from haugastol.no/rallarvegen: NOK 280 for a bike Flam-Myrdal (same fare structure applies in reverse). Flamsbana's own Rallarvegen page (https://en.flamsbana.no/rallarvegen) confirms limited bike space and that a bicycle ticket is required; contact info@flamsbana.no / +47 57 63 21 00 for edge cases. Important nuance for the plan-B decision tree: the Myrdal-Flam road section (20 km, 21 hairpins) is normally snow-free June-October per the same page, so even if the high plateau is shut, riding Myrdal-Flam after a train hop usually still works; Flamsbana is only needed if weather is truly foul or brakes/visibility make the 865 m descent unwise.

findings.title: Holdover at Finse (1,222 m): Hotel Finse 1222 and DNT Finsehytta both open mid-July; tent ban around Finse itself
findings.detail: If weather pins you at Finse, two indoor options exist. (1) Hotel Finse 1222 (https://www.hotelfinse1222.no/) is in full summer operation by mid-July 2026 (its Finsefest season opener runs 29 June-5 July 2026; online booking live), 41 rooms per Visit Norway (https://www.visitnorway.com/listings/finse-1222/175947/). (2) DNT Finsehytta, a staffed lodge 10 minutes' walk from the station, 177 beds in 2-6 bed rooms and dorms (https://en.sognefjord.no/listing/dnt-finsehytta/821234301/), booking required via hyttebestilling.dnt.no or finsehytta.booking@dnt.no, phone +47 56 52 67 32 (https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/finsehytta/english/); DNT membership cuts the rate and staffed DNT huts traditionally do not turn away arrivals in bad weather. Tent caveat: haugastol.no (https://www.haugastol.no/rallarvegen) confirms an explicit camping-ban zone around Finse sentrum (plus Haugastol-Tunga), and elsewhere tents must be 150 m from buildings, so a stormbound night at Finse means paying for a bed, not pitching the tent. Both fill in peak season; if the forecast turns midweek, book a Finse bed 2-3 days out at the same time you reserve fallback train bike spaces.

findings.title: Dovrebanen clear on 9 July 2026: summer closure ended 14 June, but Oslo S-Lillestrom reroute adds time
findings.detail: Bane NOR's official summer 2026 buss-for-tog page (https://www.banenor.no/reise-og-trafikk/aktuelt-for-reisende/buss-for-tog-sommer-2026/) lists the Dovrebanen closure as 30 May to 14 June 2026 (Eidsvoll-Loten/Storen), plus Trondheim S closed 22-28 June. SJ Norge's maintenance page (https://www.sj.no/en/maintenance/) confirms full cancellation only 30 May-15 June. No planned works hit Oslo-Vinstra on 9 July 2026. One caveat from Vy's summer 2026 page (https://www.vy.no/en/traffic/summer-engineering-works): the Gardermoen Line tunnel (Oslo S-Lillestrom) is closed 27 June-9 August, so trains run via the Hoved Line with slightly longer journey times and summer timetables apply 29 June-9 August. Trains still run (no bus substitution on this corridor), but departure times shift: re-check Entur/SJ when 2026 summer timetables load, and reserve bike spaces on the SJ Oslo-Trondheim train as they are limited and mandatory.

findings.title: Bergensbanen confirmed clear for 17 July 2026 return: no buss for tog on F4, Drammen station open
findings.detail: Vy's official summer 2026 engineering works page (https://www.vy.no/en/traffic/summer-engineering-works, live-checked June 2026) lists F4 Oslo S-Bergen (Bergensbanen) and R40 Bergen-Voss-Myrdal (Vossebanen) as running normal summer timetables with no rail replacement, and explicitly notes 'Drammen station is open for train traffic, after several summers of work'. Bane NOR's 2026 closure list (https://www.banenor.no/reise-og-trafikk/aktuelt-for-reisende/buss-for-tog-sommer-2026/) contains no Bergensbanen works; the July closures (Vestfoldbanen Drammen-Porsgrunn 4-19 July, Ostfoldbanen 4 July-2 Aug) do not touch the Bergen line. The 2025 pattern, where Drammen-area works disrupted F4 from 17 July, does not repeat in 2026. The Myrdal/Flam to Oslo return on 17 July is intact; book the bike spaces on the F4 train early as Bergensbanen bike slots sell out in July.

findings.title: Vy policy confirmed: bikes refused on Bergensbanen replacement buses; residual risk is unplanned disruption only
findings.detail: Vy's customer service FAQ (https://www.vy.no/kundeservice/sporsmal-og-svar/kan-jeg-ta-med-sykkel-pa-buss-for-tog) states verbatim that on buss for tog for Bergensbanen 'kan du dessverre ikke ta med sykkel' (bikes cannot be taken, due to limited cargo space), and on other local/regional replacement stretches carriage is not guaranteed. Since no planned works affect either travel day (see other findings), this policy only bites if an unplanned closure occurs. Mitigation: keep the evening F4 departure as a fallback on 17 July, and know that NOR-WAY's Oslo-Fagernes-Sogn express buses (nor-way.no) carry bikes subject to space as a road-based plan B from the Valdres side.

findings.title: Vinjerock confirmed 16-19 July 2026 at Eidsbugarden, sold out; no conflict with riders' 10-11 July Bygdin passage
findings.detail: Official site (https://www.vinjerock.no/) confirms Vinjerock runs 16-19 July 2026 at Eidsbugarden; festival passes are sold out per Ticketmaster Norway (https://www.ticketmaster.no/artist/vinjerock-billetter/899820). The transport page (https://www.vinjerock.no/info/transport/, updated 21 April 2026) shows festival parking opens Wednesday 15 July, Festivalekspress buses from Oslo/Bergen/Trondheim run Thursday 16 July, and no festival cars may drive to Eidsbugarden before Sunday 16:00. Riding Vinstra (9 July) to Beitostolen/Bygdin puts the riders through the area around 10-11 July, four to five days before build-up, so trail and boat congestion is a non-issue. The real effect: beds at Bygdin Fjellhotell, Fondsbu and around Beitostolen from roughly 15-19 July will be festival-loaded, which only matters if the itinerary slips by 4+ days.

findings.title: M/B Bitihorn 2026 season 26 June-13 Sep, two sailings daily, takes bikes; pre-book and avoid 16/19 July
findings.detail: Operator JVB's official page (https://jvb.no/en/boat-mb-bitihorn/, updated 3 June 2026) confirms the M/B Bitihorn runs Bygdin-Torfinnsbu-Eidsbugarden daily 26 June to 13 September 2026: from Bygdin 09:05 and 13:30, from Eidsbugarden 11:30 and 15:30. Capacity is 98 passengers; bicycles are carried as a paid add-on in the booking flow; JVB recommends booking at least 2 days ahead to guarantee a place (bookings via nor-way.no or bookings.jvb.no). For the riders this is an optional Jotunheimen detour, not on the core Mjolkevegen line, and on their likely passage dates (10-11 July) loading is normal. If anyone extends the trip toward Eidsbugarden, avoid Thursday 16 and Sunday 19 July when Vinjerock arrival/departure flows fill the boat.

findings.title: Book the Gjende boat morning departure now: 2026 sales are open, pre-booking is mandatory, and July mornings sell out
findings.detail: This is the single hardest dependency in the trip: without a morning Gjendesheim-Memurubu boat on ~Sun 12 July, the Besseggen day collapses. Verified status: 2026 ticket sales are ALREADY OPEN ('Ticket sales are open!', https://gjende.no/en/book-your-ticket-now/), season runs 6 June to 19 September 2026 with daily departures through 13 September (https://gjende.no/en/time-tables/). Pre-booking is mandatory: 'You have to book your tickets before coming' (https://gjende.no/en/some-practical-information/). Sellouts are confirmed by the operator's regional partner: 'The tour is very popular and several departures will be fully booked... even with two boats and several departures you should make sure to pre-book your ticket' (https://jotunheimen.com/plan-your-visit/gjendebaten-by-boat-on-gjende-lake/). Up to 8 departures/day run to Memurubu but the morning slots go first; exact times show only inside the booking flow at gjende.no. Price: Gjendesheim-Memurubu same-day round trip NOK 300/adult; luggage transfer NOK 100/piece. Action: book the earliest Memurubu departure for your Besseggen date this week, plus the same date one departure later as weather insurance is not possible (tickets are per departure), so instead note the official sellout fallback: hike the ridge in reverse (Gjendesheim to Memurubu on foot, boat back), which needs no morning slot. Long-stay parking is 1.7 km from the quay with a shuttle starting 30 min before first departure. Operator contact: +47 61 23 92 93, harald@gjende.no.

findings.title: Gjendesheim DNT lodge (185 beds): book the pre-Besseggen night now; official tent fallback exists at NOK 125-160 pp
findings.detail: Gjendesheim is the staging lodge for Besseggen (185 beds in 1/2/4-person rooms plus dorms) and DNT 'encourage all guests to book well in advance', with summer season running first weekend of June through week 40 (https://www.dnt.no/en/dnt-der-du-er/dnt-oslo-og-omegn/about-us/travel-trade-information/gjendesheim/, https://jotunheimen.com/accommodation/gjendesheim/). Book full-board stays at hyttebestilling.dnt.no (https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/gjendesheim/bestilling/). Verified 2026 prices from the official DNT Oslo og Omegn price list (https://www.dnt.no/globalassets/foreninger/dnt-oslo-og-omegn/priser/2026_priser_betjente-hytter_gjester_no.pdf): full board (bed, shower, breakfast, 3-course dinner, packed lunch) dorm NOK 1095 member / 1430 non-member; 4-6 bed room 1235/1630; 1-3 bed room 1315/1710. Lodging only: dorm 290/382. Tent fallback is official and cheap: 'Teltere/tilgang til hyttefasiliteter' NOK 125 member / 160 non-member per person gives campers access to hut facilities, so even if beds are gone the Besseggen night is safe with your tent. Note: DNT staffed huts do not allow eating your own food indoors or using your own stove on the premises, so budget for hut meals if tenting here (dinner NOK 470/608).

findings.title: DNT membership verdict: skip it at 2 hut nights, buy it if you add a third night or hut dinners
findings.detail: 2026 membership (Hovedmedlem) costs NOK 860/adult, confirmed in both the DNT membership page (https://www.dnt.no/medlem/) and the official 2026 price PDF (https://www.dnt.no/globalassets/foreninger/dnt-oslo-og-omegn/priser/2026_priser_betjente-hytter_gjester_no.pdf). Member savings on this trip's two likely staffed-hut nights (Gjendesheim + Finsehytta, identical standard DNT pricing per https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/finsehytta/english/booking/): full board dorm saves NOK 335/night, 4-6 or 1-3 bed rooms save NOK 395/night. Two full-board nights = NOK 670-790 saved per person vs the 860 fee: a net loss of 70-190. It flips positive with any one of: a third staffed-hut night (weather buffer day), one extra member-priced dinner (saves 138) plus breakfast (saves 63), or any DNT hut use later in 2026 (membership covers the calendar year, discounts at ~600 huts, https://www.dnt.no/medlem/medlemsfordeler/rabatt-pa-hytter/). Recommendation: don't buy purely for this itinerary as planned; buy if you want the flexibility of an extra hut night, which on this route in mid-July is a realistic scenario.

findings.title: Finse night: Finsehytta opens 30 June and Finse 1222 peaks in July; book one now, wild-camp fallback is legal but exposed at 1222 m
findings.nearStop: Finse (Rallarvegen, no road access, train or bike only)
findings.detail: Finse has exactly two indoor options and no road access, so demand concentrates hard. Finsehytta (DNT, ~150 beds): summer season runs 30 June to end of week 40, so your ~16 July night is in full-service season (https://www.dnt.no/en/cabins/betjente/finsehytta/opening-hours/); book at hyttebestilling.dnt.no or finsehytta.booking@dnt.no, +47 56 52 67 32; 2026 full board from NOK 1095 member / 1430 non-member in dorms (https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/finsehytta/english/booking/). Hotel Finse 1222: open year-round, books via booking.hotelfinse1222.no, +47 56 52 71 00 (https://www.hotelfinse1222.no/); TripAdvisor lists July-September as its busiest period (https://www.tripadvisor.com/Hotel_Review-g795951-d1478306-Reviews-Hotel_Finse_1222-Finse_Ulvik_Municipality_Hardanger_Hordaland_Western_Norway.html). Neither published live July 2026 occupancy, so treat four weeks out as the booking deadline, not a comfort margin: check hyttebestilling.dnt.no and booking.hotelfinse1222.no today, both show live availability. Fallback: allemannsretten wild camping around Finsevatnet is legal and free, and Finsehytta charges the standard DNT camper rate for facility access, but you are at 1222 m on the Hardangervidda edge where mid-July nights can hit low single digits with wind: fine for your kit, just not a night to discover a worn tent seam.

findings.title: Flam endgame: Naeroyfjord cruise sells out days ahead (NOK 520-595 one-way) and Flam beds go first; per-night book-now vs tent plan
findings.detail: Cruise: two operators run Flam-Gudvangen. Norway's Best/The Fjords (electric vessels, year-round, books at https://www.norwaysbest.com/en/flam/things-to-do/fjord-cruise-naeroyfjord) and Lustrabaatane (classic boats, Apr-Oct, summer departures 08:30/11:00/13:30/16:00 from each end, arrive 15 min early, https://en.lustrabaatane.no/flam-gudvangen). 2026 one-way pricing NOK 520-595, Lustrabaatane family ticket NOK 1300 (https://flamtravelguide.com/ferry-flam-gudvangen-naeroyfjord-cruise/). Same source confirms 'certain departures can and will sell out on busy days' in summer and both operators' tickets are refundable to 48h before departure (https://www.scenicandsavvy.com/flam-to-gudvangen-ferry/), so booking now carries no downside: book it this week for your Flam day. Beds: Flam accommodation 'is limited and very likely to sell out in the summer' (https://thehiddennorth.com/where-to-stay-in-flam-from-a-tour-leaders-perspective/), with Fretheim Hotel observed sold out months ahead in past seasons; Flam Camping & Hostel has hostel beds NOK 550-750 and cabins NOK 900-1790 (https://www.flaam-camping.no/), with Aurland 10 km away as overflow. Per-night plan, two riders: Thu 9 Jul Vinstra area: tent/campsite, no booking. Fri 10 Jul Beitostolen: tent fine, book a bed only if wanted. Sat 11 Jul Gjendesheim: BOOK NOW (tent-at-hut fallback NOK 125-160 pp). Sun 12 Jul post-Besseggen (note: Sunday, groceries shut, buy food Saturday or eat at the hut): BOOK BOAT NOW, bed/tent per Saturday plan. Mon 13 Jul Vaset: tent/campsite. Tue 14 Jul Gol: tent/campsite. Wed 15 Jul Geilo or Haugastol: tent fine, beds optional. Thu 16 Jul Finse: BOOK NOW (wild-camp fallback, exposed). Fri 17 Jul Flam: BOOK NOW for bed and cruise (campsite tent pitch as fallback). Net: four book-now items (Gjende boat, Gjendesheim, Finse, Flam bed+cruise), everything else tents safely.

findings.title: Gjendesheim: only storage is an UNLOCKED ski room, no bike facilities advertised
findings.detail: DNT Gjendesheim's official 'Hyttebesoket fra A-A' page is explicit: baggage for people heading into the mountains goes in the skistall (ski room), which 'star ulast hele dognet' (stands unlocked around the clock) and 'all oppbevaring skjer pa eget ansvar' (all storage at your own risk) - https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/gjendesheim/om-hytta/hyttebesoket-fra-a-a/. No bike parking, bike shed or lockers are mentioned there, on the lodge's own pages (https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/gjendesheim/om-hytta/), on besseggen.net's facility rundown (https://besseggen.net/overnatting/gjendesheim-turisthytte/?lang=en), or in SykkelValdres's Gjendesheim-by-bike guide (https://sykkelvaldres.no/da/guides/gjendesheim-besseggen-sykkel-valdres). Plan for the 6-8h Besseggen day: panniers into the skistall (valuables in your daypack), bikes locked together and to a fixed object near the lodge. If you overnight at Gjendesheim before/after the hike, email gjendesheim@dnt.no (+47 61 23 89 10) and ask staff to put bags behind reception; that is the only supervised option. Note the trailhead area is busy with day-tourists in July (shuttle buses run from Reinsvangen parking 2 km east), so this is one of the two points on the route where a real lock earns its weight.

findings.title: Finse: no published lockable bike storage at Finse 1222 or Finsehytta, but lowest-risk stop on the route
findings.detail: Neither Hotel Finse 1222 (https://www.hotelfinse1222.no/) nor DNT Finsehytta (https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/finsehytta/rallarvegen/ and the practical-tips page https://www.dnt.no/hytter/betjente/finsehytta/english/hei2/rallarvegen/tips-og-praktisk-informasjon-rallarvegen/) publishes anything about a sykkelbod or lockable bike room as of June 2026. What is documented: Finse 1222 runs its own bike department ('Sykkel 1222') from the red building at the east end of the station platform for its Rallarvegen rental fleet (https://old.finse1222.no/en/rallarvegen-the-navvy-road/), and Tripadvisor lists 'Baggage Storage' among the hotel's amenities (https://www.tripadvisor.com/Hotel_Feature-g795951-d1478306-zft1-Hotel_Finse_1222.html). Context matters more than facilities here: Finse has no road access at all (train-only, 1222 m), so a thief cannot drive a bike away, and in July dozens of rental bikes routinely stand in open racks overnight. Practical plan: lock the two bikes to each other outside whichever you stay at, bring panniers inside your room/dorm, and email ahead to confirm inside storage if you want it (finsehytta.booking@dnt.no / +47 56 52 67 32 for the DNT hut; the hotel via hotelfinse1222.no contact form).

findings.title: Flam: verified 24/7 self-service luggage lockers at the Visitor Centre, NOK 150-200/day, max 3 days
findings.detail: Confirmed on the operator's own page (Norway's Best, who run the port and station area): a luggage room with self-service lockers sits 'next to the Customer Center and the station building in Flam', boxes 'available 24/7', several sizes, 'payment made by credit card on site', 'current prices vary from NOK 150-200/per day, depending on the size', 'rental time is max. 3 days' (https://www.norwaysbest.com/en/flam/luggage-storage-in-flam, contact info@norwaysbest.com / +47 57 63 14 00). This cleanly solves the panniers problem for the fjord-cruise and Stegastein day: strip the bikes, lockers take the bags, larger box sizes should fit two riders' panniers in one box (~NOK 200 total). Bikes do not fit in the lockers, so they get locked outside (see the Flam risk note). The lockers are 50 m from the cruise pontoon, so you can drop bags minutes before boarding.

findings.title: Flam is the one real theft-risk node: no documented bike shed at Flam Camping, leave bikes at the campsite not the pier
findings.detail: Flam Camping & Hostel advertises 'a warm welcome to bikers and hikers' but its site documents no lockable bike shed or storage room (https://www.flaam-camping.no/accommodation/hostel/, main site https://www.flaam-camping.no/). Unlike everywhere else on your route, Flam concentrates cruise-ship and coach traffic (tens of thousands of pass-through visitors in July), so treat it as the highest-exposure point of the trip. Two mitigations: (1) the campsite is ~400 m from the pier, so during the cruise/Stegastein leave bikes locked inside the campsite grounds (next to your pitched tent or reception) rather than in the public racks at the harbour, with panniers either in the tent or in the station lockers; (2) email ahead (book via flaam-camping.no) to ask whether they can put the bikes in a locked room overnight - small Norwegian campsites usually accommodate this on request even when not advertised. If you take a cheap hotel instead (Flamsbrygga, Heimly), ask the same question at booking; Tripadvisor-listed amenities suggest baggage rooms are standard but bike rooms are not.
findings.nearStop: Flam Camping & Hostel, ~400 m from pier

findings.title: Lock strategy: one mid-weight lock shared between two bikes covers the whole route
findings.detail: Nothing on this route offers guaranteed lockable bike storage, but exposure is limited to two windows: Gjendesheim (6-8 h unattended at a busy trailhead) and Flam (cruise port). Everywhere else (Vinstra, Beitostolen, Vaset, Gol, Geilo, Haugastol, Finse, Myrdal) is rural Norway or road-free mountain, where touring practice is to immobilise rather than fortify. Standard bicycle-touring guidance for low-risk settings: lock both bikes together and to a fixed object so neither can be wheeled away, run the lock through frame and rear wheel, and keep valuables off the bike ('one of three things on your bike at all times: your hand, your butt or a good lock' - https://bicycletravellers.com/learn/bike-security.html; same consensus in r/bicycletouring: a small cable lock for camp/cafe stops, valuables in a daypack - https://www.reddit.com/r/bicycletouring/comments/1kzyfih/where_to_secure_your_bike_overnight/). Concrete packing call: one folding or compact chain lock (~600-900 g, e.g. Abus Bordo class) carried by one rider and shared, plus a ~50 g loop cable to daisy-chain helmets/saddles and pin panniers at cafe stops. Skip carrying two heavy U-locks: against the route's actual risk profile the second lock buys almost nothing for 1+ kg over nine days of climbing.
findings.nearStop: Applies route-wide; critical at Gjendesheim and Flam
